title: Eliminate false sharing between cores
description: Keep independently-written variables on separate cache lines so cores do not invalidate each other's lines on every write.
body: Use when a multithreaded workload scales poorly despite independent per-thread data. Do not place hot counters or per-thread state adjacently within one cache line. Actions: identify variables written by different cores, pad each to its own line (alignas(64) or per-thread arrays spaced by line size), and confirm coherence traffic drops. Validation signals include a perf report showing fewer HITM / cache-line-bounce events and near-linear scaling after the fix. Risk boundary: over-padding wastes cache and can hurt single-threaded locality; verify the variables are genuinely contended before spacing them out, and re-measure scaling rather than assuming.
